danish-chicken-liver-pate.name: Danish Chicken Liver Pate
instructions: Preheat oven to 199°C (200 degrees C).
Mix flour and water together in a bowl until a thin paste forms, adding more water as needed.
Combine flour mixture, chicken livers, cream, onion, eggs, butter, salt, nutmeg, and black pepper in a food processor; process until blended and smooth, 30 to 45 seconds.
Transfer liver mixture to three 7x4-inch disposable pans. Cover pans with aluminum foil and place in a baking pan. Pour enough water into the baking pan to come up halfway up the sides.

Bake in the preheated oven for 25 minutes. Remove foil and continue baking until cooked through, about 25 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 74°C (74 degrees C). Cool to room temperature. Cover with lids and refrigerate.

darcys-squash-pie.name: Darcy's Squash Pie
instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C). Press pie pastry into a 9-inch pie plate.
Beat evaporated milk, squash, sugars, eggs, cinnamon, vanilla, and salt together in a large bowl using an electric mixer until smooth; pour into pie crust.
Bake in the preheated oven for 10 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 177°C (175 degrees C) and continue baking until the center of pie is set, about 30 minutes more.

instructions: 1. Take it in a glass bowl.
2. Half sweet spoon black pepper and half sweet spoon add salt and knead.

deep-sea-salmon-with-green-sauce-lezzet.name: Deep Sea Salmon With Green Sauce
deep-sea-salmon-with-green-sauce-lezzet.original_name: Green Sauce Open Sea Salmon  u
instructions: 1. The recipes that can be easily applied in the upper row.

deer-tongues-teleculinaria.name: Deer Tongues
instructions: Beat the sugar very well with the butter until you get a cream. Add the eggs, one by one and beat well between each addition. Add the milk and wrap. Finally add the flour and beat until it is homogeneous.
Line an oven tray with vegetable paper. Place the previous preparation in a pastry bag with smooth mouth and small tent strips on the board, as if they were tongues, leaving some space between them.
Tap the tray on the counter to release the air accumulated in the dough. Take the tray to the oven, preheated to 200oC, for about 5 minutes. Take it off, take it off the paper and let it cool.

delicious-venezuelan-catalinas-comedera.name: Delicious Venezuelan Catalinas
instructions: It starts the recipe of the Catalines and puts half a cup of water next to the pieces of paper and spicy nails into a pot with medium heat.
Cook and get mixed up till the paper's dissolved. Turn off once you have a malted mix with consistency.
Apaga, retira los clavos especia y reserva.
Now, into a bowl incorporates the flour. Then add the ground cinnamon, bake powder and ground spicy nail. Mix very well until dry materials are integrated.
Procede e incorpora la mantequilla y mezcla hasta que unan a los ingredientes secos y te quede una masa medio arenosa.
Continue and pour the paper into the mixture by the ginger. Mix very well till a smooth and homogenous mass remains. He's got to be manageable.
Preheat the oven at 180 ° C
Engrasa una bandeja con un poco de aceite o mantequilla.
Wet your hands with oil and divide mass into medium small balls.
Later. Flow a little and place onto the plate leaving separation.
Una vez colocadas todas, hornea por aproximadamente 30 minutos. Al sacar del horno, despega de la bandeja y deja reposar.
Now, they'll be ready to enjoy.

donuts-fried-doughnuts-teleculinaria.name: Donuts - Fried Doughnuts
instructions: Prepare the dough: mix the flour with the yeast. Add the milk, soft butter, yolks, sugar and spices and start kneading. Add the pinch of salt, work the dough until it is very homogeneous and elastic and then put it in a floured bowl. Let it light until it folds.

Stretch the dough on the floured bench until it is 1.5 cm thick. Cut the doughnuts, with the help of a ten-inch diameter cutter. Then cut the center with a smaller cutter (about 3 cm in diameter). Have them on a board and let it take off again for about 30 minutes.
After the specified time, fry in warm and abundant oil, remove them and let them drip on absorbent paper.

Prepare the cover: mix the sugar with cocoa and add the milk, in yarn and always stirring, until you get a smooth preparation. Cover the surface of the doughnuts with the cream and let it dry. Sprinkle with chocolate chips before serving.

doreens-pork-and-sauerkraut.name: Doreen's Pork And Sauerkraut
instructions: Preheat oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).

Spray a large roasting pan with cooking spray. Place pork roast in the pan and arrange sauerkraut around it. Place apple wedges on top of the sauerkraut. Sprinkle brown sugar over roast and apples. Cover roasting pan with aluminum foil.
Bake roast in the preheated oven until roast is slightly pink in the center, about 2 hours. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 63°C (63 degrees C).
Remove roast from oven and allow to rest in a warm area before slicing, about 10 minutes.

dorito-crusted-fried-mac-n-cheese-balls.name: Dorito Crusted Fried Mac 'n Cheese Balls
instructions: Add the Doritos to the bowl of a food processor. Pulse until finely ground and the texture resembles panko. Pour into a shallow bowl and set aside.
Place 1 cup flour and the beaten eggs into 2 separate shallow bowls. Set aside.
In a large bowl, mix together the leftover mac and cheese, cheddar, and mozzarella until well combined. If the mixture does not stick together, add ¼ cup flour and mix until evenly combined.
Roll about ⅓ cup of the mac and cheese mixture into a compact ball, tightly pressing it together with your hands. Repeat with the remaining mac and cheese mixture until you have 9 balls.
Transfer the balls to the fridge to stiffen up for about 20 minutes.
Working one at a time, roll the balls in the flour, beaten eggs, then crushed Doritos. Shake off any excess and set the coated ball on a parchment-lined sheet tray. Repeat with the remaining balls.
Heat the canola oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot fitted with a deep-fry thermometer over medium heat until it reaches 177°C (180°C).
Working in batches of 2–3 at a time, fry the balls in the hot oil for 2-3 minutes, until golden brown. Drain on paper towels and sprinkle with salt.
Serve hot and enjoy!

double-chocolate-tart-with-black-pepper-.name: Double Chocolate Tart With Black Pepper Ice Cream

ingredients.item: Chocolate wafer cookies (such as nabisco famous chocolate wafers; about 28 cookies)

instructions: Bring cream, milk, 1/2 cup sugar, and salt to a boil in a large heavy saucepan over medium heat, stirring to dissolve sugar; remove from heat. Whisk egg yolks and remaining 1/2 cup sugar in a large bowl to blend; gradually whisk in hot milk mixture and return to saucepan. Stir over low heat until custard thickens and your finger leaves a path on the back of a spoon when drawn across, about 6 minutes. Pour through a fine-mesh strainer into a medium bowl. Stir in pepper. Set bowl over a large bowl of ice water; stir until cold.
Process custard in an ice cream maker according to manufacturer's instructions. Transfer to a container, cover, and freeze. DO AHEAD Can be made 3 days ahead.

Preheat oven to 250°. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Whisk sugar, egg white, and salt in a medium bowl just until bubbles form. Stir in hazelnuts. Spread out on prepared sheet. Bake, stirring every 10 minutes, until nuts are golden brown and caramelized, about 50 minutes. DO AHEAD Can be made 1 day ahead. Store airtight at room temperature.

Preheat oven to 325°. Coat tart pan with nonstick spray. Finely grind cookies in a food processor. Drizzle butter over; pulse until blended. Press evenly onto bottom and up sides of pan. Bake until firm, about 15 minutes. Let cool completely.

Place chocolate in a medium bowl. Bring cream to a boil in a small saucepan. Pour over chocolate; stir until melted. Pour filling over cooled crust. Freeze until firm, at least 2 hours and up to 2 days.

Remove the tart pan side. Cut tart into wedges, working quickly as tart will become gooey as it sits; place 1 piece on each plate. Serve with a scoop of black pepper ice cream and garnish with candied hazelnuts.

double-chocolate-candy-cane-kiss-cookies.name: Double Chocolate-candy Cane Kiss Cookies

ingredients.item: White chocolate kisses with candy cane stripes

instructions: Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C). Line 2 baking sheets with parchment paper.

Beat butter in a large bowl with an electric mixer for 30 seconds. Add sugar, brown sugar, and baking soda; beat until well mixed. Mix in eggs, melted chocolate, and vanilla extract. Gradually beat in flour and cocoa powder until well combined.
Drop rounded teaspoonfuls of dough 2 inches apart onto the prepared baking sheets.

Bake in the preheated oven until edges begin to firm up, about 7 minutes. Remove from the oven and add 1 white chocolate kiss to the center of each cookie, pressing down just slightly. Return to the oven and bake for approximately 1 minute. Cool on the baking sheets for 1 minute before removing to a wire rack to cool completely.

double-chocolate-peppermint-crunch-cooki.name: Double Chocolate-peppermint Crunch Cookies

ingredients.item: Bittersweet chocolate chips (do not exceed 61% cacao; 15 to 16 ounces)
Candy canes or 16 red-and-white-striped hard peppermint candies

instructions: Preheat oven to 191°C. Line 2 large rimmed baking sheets with parchment paper. Stir 2 cups chocolate chips in medium metal bowl set over saucepan of simmering water until melted and smooth. Measure 2/3 cup melted chocolate; transfer to small metal bowl and reserve for drizzling.

Whisk flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, espresso powder, and salt in medium bowl. Using electric mixer, beat butter in large bowl until creamy. Add sugar and both extracts; beat until smooth. Add eggs; beat to blend. Beat in melted chocolate from medium bowl. Add dry ingredients; beat just to blend. Stir in remaining 1/2 cup chocolate chips. Measure 1 level tablespoonful dough; roll dough between palms to form ball. Place on prepared baking sheet. Repeat with remaining dough, spacing cookies 11/2 inches apart.

Bake cookies until cracked all over and tester inserted into center comes out with large moist crumbs attached, 8 to 9 minutes. Cool cookies on baking sheet 5 minutes. Transfer cookies on parchment paper to racks to cool completely.

Rewarm reserved 2/3 cup chocolate over small saucepan of simmering water. Using fork, drizzle chocolate over cookies. Sprinkle crushed candy canes over, arranging some pieces with red parts showing. Chill just until chocolate sets, about 20 minutes. DO AHEAD Can be made 3 weeks ahead. Store airtight in freezer. Bring to room temperature before serving.

dried-chile-salsa.name: Dried Chile Salsa
instructions: Toast chiles in a dry medium skillet over high heat, turning often, until fragrant, about 3 minutes. Let cool slightly, then remove stems and seeds. Place chiles, onion, garlic, salt, and 1 1/2 cups water in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer until very soft, 15–20 minutes. Purée chile mixture in a blender until smooth. Strain into a medium bowl and mix in vinegar.
DO AHEAD: Salsa can be made 4 days ahead. Cover and chill.

dried-fruit-english-muffin-lezzet.name: Dried Fruit English Muffin

ingredients.item: Half-water cup with coarse broken walnut inside
